data.title: Stocks fall, currencies muted as US tariff worries linger
data.description:
Key points:
  • EM stocks down 0.6%, but set for mild weekly gains
  • Thailand's stock exchange suspends trading after Myanmar earthquake
  • U.S. PCE data due at 1230 GMT

Most emerging market stocks and currencies weakened on Friday as U.S. President Donald Trump's latest volley of tariffs hit risk-taking, with the focus now on a U.S. inflation reading for cues into the Federal Reserve's interest rate path.

MSCI's index for emerging market stocks CBOE:EFS dipped 0.6% as bourses in China , Hong Kong and India - some of the index heavyweights - declined in the wider risk-off move.

Despite some weakness towards the latter half of March, EM stocks were set for their best monthly showing since September 2024, aided by a recovery in Chinese and Indian shares, along with a weaker U.S. dollar lending support.

A gauge for Eastern European equities (.MIME00000PUS), despite falling 0.8% on the day, was on pace for a more than 11% jump this month, benefitting from prospects of increased fiscal spending in Europe and growing chances of a ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.

Earlier in the day, Russian news agencies reported that President Vladimir Putin suggested Ukraine be placed under a form of temporary administration to allow for new elections and the signature of key accords to reach a settlement in the war.

Most currencies in emerging Europe and Africa were muted, while stocks languished amid the tariff uncertainty.

South Africa's rand appreciated 0.4% in choppy trading against the dollar, while Hungary's forint lost 0.3% against the euro.

Back in Asia, the Stock Exchange of Thailand suspended all trading activities for the afternoon session after neighbouring Myanmar was rocked by a strong earthquake, the tremors of which were felt in Thailand's capital, Bangkok.

Korean automakers Hyundai Motor and Kia extended their slump after Trump imposed a 25% tariff on imported cars and light trucks starting next week.

The focus is now on U.S. reciprocal tariffs due to be announced on April 2. Trump indicated that the measures may not be the like-for-like levies he has been vowing to impose.

"As Trump's tariffs continue to accumulate and as other countries retaliate, the risk of a more significant stagflationary shock has increased," Rabobank analysts said.

INFLATION AND RATES

The U.S. Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data, the Fed's preferred gauge for inflation, is due at 1230 GMT and is expected to show the core PCE rising to 2.7% on a yearly basis.

Worries that Trump's tariff could stoke inflation have gripped markets of late, with the spotlight on how the Fed could respond in terms of its monetary policy.

Traders have priced in at least two 25-basis-points rate cuts by the end of 2025 and are seeing a more than 57% chance of a third such reduction, according to LSEG data.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Ukrainian official says discussions continue around draft minerals deal with US

** India offers US tariff cuts on farm imports, eyes trade success, government sources say

For TOP NEWS across emerging markets

data.title: Bulgaria Tourist Arrivals Rebound in February
data.description:
The number of tourist arrivals in Bulgaria rose by 3.1% year-on-year to 720.6 thousand in February 2025, rebounding from a 0.3% fall in the previous month.

An increase was registered in trips with professional purposes (10.5%), those with holiday and recreation purposes (3.4%), and those with other purposes (0.8%).

The EU constituted 53.6% of the total visits to Bulgaria, with Romania (44.3%) being the largest contributor, followed by Greece (25.6%).

Meanwhile, arrivals from the other European countries accounted for 39.4% of the total, mainly from Turkey (55.2%).

data.title: Topcon: KKR Plans to Indirectly Hold Majority Interest in Topcon >7732.TO
Topcon: JICC Plans to Indirectly Hold Voting Rights in Topcon >7732.TO
KKR and JIC take Japan's Topcon private for $2.31 billion
data.description:
U.S.-based private equity firm KKR and Japanese state-backed fund Japan Investment Corp (JIC) will acquire medical gear maker Topcon in a tender offer buyout worth 348 billion yen ($2.31 billion), KKR said on Friday.

Private equity deals in Japan have been booming as corporate governance reforms and increasingly vocal activist shareholders push more companies to go private and carve out non-core business operations.

The tender offer price of 3,300 per share marks a 5.4% premium on Topcon's closing price of 3,130 yen on Friday and a 99.5% premium over the simple average closing price of Topcon's stock for the 12 months up to December 9, 2024, KKR said.

($1 = 150.5200 yen)

Diversified Energy Places $300 Million Notes Due 2029
data.description:
Diversified Energy Company placed $300 million of new senior secured notes due April 2029, with a fixed annual coupon of 9.75%.

The London-listed energy company will use the net proceeds to repay debt and fund general business needs, according to a Friday filing.

DNB Markets was the manager and bookrunner for the offering.

data.title: Fujairah Cement Industries Resumes Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Fujairah Cement Industries (ADX:FCI) recommenced trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange on Friday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started Thursday and was lifted after the cement manufacturer published the results of its meeting.

data.title: Italy business and consumer morale slump in March as outlook worsens
data.description:
Morale among Italian businesses and consumers slumped in March, data showed on Friday, casting a shadow over already weak growth prospects for the euro zone's third largest economy.

National statistics bureau ISTAT's composite business confidence index, combining surveys of the manufacturing, retail, construction and services sectors, fell to 93.3 from 94.7 the previous month, posting the lowest reading since November last year.

The decline was driven by a steep drop in service sector morale. Manufacturing also registered a decline, while construction sector confidence picked up and sentiment among retailers was broadly stable.

Consumer confidence plunged this month to 95.0 from 98.8 in February, ISTAT said, far below a median forecast of 98.8 in a Reuters' poll of seven analysts and posting the lowest level since November 2023.

The Italian economy grew by 0.7% in 2024, data showed this month, below the government's 1% target and registering the same growth rate as in 2023. Most analysts expect a similar modest expansion this year, again below Rome's target of 1.2%.

Recent economic data has been mostly weak, and the outlook is clouded by geopolitical tensions, the prospect of U.S. trade tariffs and the difficulties faced by Rome in deploying COVID-19 pandemic recovery funds received from the European Union.

data.title: Banco Santander: Dividend to be Voted at the AGM Expected on April 4
Eurozone Consumer Inflation Expectations Steady
data.description:
The Eurozone's median inflation expectations for the next 12 months remained unchanged at 2.6% in February 2025, the same as in January while inflation expectations for three years ahead also steadied at 2.4%.

Perceived inflation over the previous 12 months was 3.1%, suggesting consumers do expect a slowdown in inflation.

Uncertainty about inflation expectations over the next 12 months decreased slightly to its lowest level since January 2022.

Younger respondents (aged 18-34) continued to report lower inflation perceptions and expectations than older respondents (those aged 35-54 and 55-70).

Meanwhile, consumers’ nominal income growth expectations over the next 12 months increased to 1% in February from 0.9% in January.

On the other hand, economic growth expectations for the next 12 months were more negative (-1.2% vs -1.1%) and expectations for the unemployment rate increased to 10.5% from 10.4%.

data.title: Jefferies Adjusts Maoyan Entertainment's Price Target to HK$9.20 From HK$9.80, Keeps at Buy
data.description: Maoyan Entertainment has an average rating of buy and mean price target of HK$9.71, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Jio Financial Services Invests INR850 Million in Jio Payments Bank
data.description:
Jio Financial Services invested 850 million Indian rupees in Jio Payments Bank by subscribing to its 85 million equity shares, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The investment increases Jio Financial Services' stake in Jio Payments Bank JPBL to 85.04% from 82.17%.

The deal has been approved by the Reserve Bank of India, and no further regulatory approvals are required, the filing said.
